𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Four — Part One

The Torah — Fourth Book

BAMIDBAR

In the Wilderness

Chapters 1 through 19

The Census — The Camp Order — The Levites — The Spies — The Forty Years

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QNuma (4Q23) · 4QNumb (4Q27) · 4QNumc (4Q28) · 4QNumd (4Q29)

4QNume (4Q30) · 4QNumf (4Q31) · 4QNumg (4Q32) · 4QNumh (4Q33)

4QNumi (4Q34) · 4QNumj (4Q35) · 4QNumk (4Q36) · 4QNuml (4Q37)

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Beta Israel Ancient Canon

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitutions

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

THE CENSUS AND THE CAMP — CHAPTERS 1 THROUGH 4

BAMIDBAR — CHAPTER 1 The First Census — The Twelve Tribes Numbered

1:1 YHWH spoke to Moshe in the wilderness of Sinai, in the tent of meeting, on the first day of the second month, in the second year after they had come out of the land of Egypt, saying:

1:2 Take a census of all the congregation of the people of Yisra'EL, by clans, by fathers' houses, according to the number of names, every male, head by head.

1:3 From twenty years old and upward, all in Yisra'EL who are able to go to war, you and Aharon shall list them, company by company.

1:17 Then Moshe and Aharon took these men who had been named,

1:18 And on the first day of the second month, they assembled the whole congregation together, who registered themselves by clans, by fathers' houses, according to the number of names from twenty years old and upward, head by head.

1:20 The people of Re'uven, Yisra'EL's firstborn, their generations, by their clans, by their fathers' houses, according to the number of names, head by head, every male from twenty years old and upward, all who were able to go to war:

1:21 Their listing in the tribe of Re'uven was 46,500.

1:26 Of the people of Yehudah, their generations, by their clans, by their fathers' houses, according to the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, every man able to go to war:

1:27 Their listing in the tribe of Yehudah was 74,600.

1:44 These are those who were listed, whom Moshe and Aharon listed with the help of the chiefs of Yisra'EL, twelve men, each representing his fathers' house.

1:45 So all those listed of the people of Yisra'EL, by their fathers' houses, from twenty years old and upward, every man able to go to war in Yisra'EL —

1:46 All those listed were 603,550.

1:47 But the Levites were not listed along with them by their ancestral tribe.

1:50 But appoint the Levites over the Mishkan of the testimony, and over all its furnishings, and over all that belongs to it. They are to carry the Mishkan and all its furnishings, and they shall take care of it and shall camp around the Mishkan.

1:51 When the Mishkan is to set out, the Levites shall take it down, and when the Mishkan is to be pitched, the Levites shall set it up. And if any outsider comes near, he shall be put to death.

1:53 But the Levites shall camp around the Mishkan of the testimony, so that there may be no wrath on the congregation of the people of Yisra'EL. And the Levites shall keep guard over the Mishkan of the testimony.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QNuma and 4QNumb preserve portions of Chapter 1. The census figures and the Levitical exemption are attested in the Cave 4 Numbers manuscripts. Bamidbar is one of the most extensively preserved books in the Qumran corpus — twelve separate manuscripts identified.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 1 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] 4QNuma presents some variant readings in the census figures. Where the DSS and Masoretic differ in numerical detail, the DSS reading is followed and the Masoretic variant noted.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1:46 — all those listed were 603,550. The census number across the Torah is one of the most discussed textual questions in the ancient record. The number as stated would represent an Exodus community of over two million people when women and children are included. The DSS manuscripts preserve the same figures as the Masoretic. The ancient record states the numbers as given. The theological significance — the fulfillment of YHWH's promise to multiply the offspring of Avraham as the stars of heaven — is the frame within which the census numbers function in the text.

BAMIDBAR — CHAPTER 2 The Arrangement of the Camp — Each Tribe in Its Position

2:1 YHWH spoke to Moshe and Aharon, saying:

2:2 The people of Yisra'EL shall camp each by his own standard, with the banners of their fathers' houses. They shall camp facing the tent of meeting on every side.

2:3 Those to camp on the east side toward the sunrise shall be of the standard of the camp of Yehudah by their companies. The chief of the people of Yehudah being Nakhshon the son of Amminadav.

2:10 On the south side shall be the standard of the camp of Re'uven by their companies, the chief of the people of Re'uven being Elitzur the son of Shede'ur.

2:17 Then the tent of meeting shall set out, with the camp of the Levites in the midst of the camps — as they camp, so they shall set out, each in position, standard by standard.

2:18 On the west side shall be the standard of the camp of Efrayim by their companies, the chief of the people of Efrayim being Elishama the son of Ammihud.

2:25 On the north side shall be the standard of the camp of Dan by their companies, the chief of the people of Dan being Ahi'ezer the son of Ammishaddai.

2:32 These are the people of Yisra'EL as listed by their fathers' houses. All those listed in the camps by their companies were 603,550.

2:33 But the Levites were not listed among the people of Yisra'EL, as YHWH commanded Moshe.

2:34 Thus did the people of Yisra'EL. According to all that YHWH commanded Moshe, so they camped by their standards, and so they set out, each one in his clan, according to his fathers' house.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QNuma preserves portions of Chapter 2.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 2 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The camp arrangement places the tent of meeting at the absolute center, with the Levites encircling it and the twelve tribes arranged in four groups of three around the perimeter — east, south, west, north. The camp of Yisra'EL in the wilderness is designed as a sacred space with YHWH's dwelling at its geographic and theological center. Every tribe's position relative to the Mishkan is fixed by YHWH's command. The order of march follows the order of camp. The wilderness community is structured around the presence of YHWH, not around military convenience or tribal seniority.

BAMIDBAR — CHAPTER 3 The Levites Assigned — The Firstborn Redeemed

3:1 These are the generations of Aharon and Moshe at the time when YHWH spoke with Moshe on Mount Sinai.

3:5 And YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

3:6 Bring the tribe of Levi near, and set them before Aharon the priest, that they may minister to him.

3:7 They shall keep guard over him and over the whole congregation before the tent of meeting, as they minister at the Mishkan.

3:9 And you shall give the Levites to Aharon and his sons — they are wholly given to him from among the people of Yisra'EL.

3:11 And YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

3:12 Behold, I have taken the Levites from among the people of Yisra'EL instead of every firstborn who opens the womb among the people of Yisra'EL. The Levites shall be mine,

3:13 For all the firstborn are mine. On the day that I struck down all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, I consecrated for my own all the firstborn in Yisra'EL, both of man and of beast. They shall be mine — I am YHWH.

3:14 And YHWH spoke to Moshe in the wilderness of Sinai, saying:

3:15 List the sons of Levi, by fathers' houses and by clans — every male from a month old and upward you shall list.

3:39 All those listed among the Levites, whom Moshe and Aharon listed at the commandment of YHWH, by clans, all the males from a month old and upward, were 22,000.

3:40 And YHWH said to Moshe: List all the firstborn males of the people of Yisra'EL, from a month old and upward, taking the number of their names.

3:44 And YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

3:45 Take the Levites instead of all the firstborn among the people of Yisra'EL, and the livestock of the Levites instead of their livestock. The Levites shall be mine — I am YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QNuma and 4QNumb preserve portions of Chapter 3.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 3 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 3:12-13 — I have taken the Levites instead of every firstborn. The tenth plague in Shemot established the firstborn of Yisra'EL as belonging to YHWH — consecrated on the night the firstborn of Egypt died. In Bamidbar the Levites are designated as the substitute for every firstborn male of Yisra'EL. The tribe of Levi serves before YHWH in place of every firstborn son in every tribe. The covenant claim from the night of the Pesach is redeemed through the Levitical assignment. Nothing in the Torah is disconnected — the Pesach night in Shemot creates the Levitical system in Bamidbar.

BAMIDBAR — CHAPTER 4 The Duties of the Levitical Clans — Kehat, Gershon, Merari

4:1 YHWH spoke to Moshe and Aharon, saying:

4:2 Take a census of the sons of Kehat from among the sons of Levi, by their clans and their fathers' houses,

4:3 From thirty years old up to fifty years old, all who can come on duty, to do the work in the tent of meeting.

4:4 This is the service of the sons of Kehat in the tent of meeting — the most holy things.

4:15 And when Aharon and his sons have finished covering the holy furnishings and all the holy utensils, when the camp is to set out, after that the sons of Kehat shall come to carry these, but they must not touch the holy things, lest they die.

4:17 And YHWH spoke to Moshe and Aharon, saying:

4:18 Let not the tribe of the clans of Kehat be destroyed from among the Levites,

4:19 But deal thus with them, that they may live and not die when they come near to the most holy things: Aharon and his sons shall go in and appoint each of them to his task and to his burden.

4:21 YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

4:22 Take a census of the sons of Gershon also, by their fathers' houses and by their clans.

4:29 As for the sons of Merari, you shall list them by their clans and their fathers' houses.

4:48 Their listing was 8,580. According to the commandment of YHWH through Moshe, each one with his task of serving or carrying — thus they were listed by him, as YHWH commanded Moshe.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QNuma preserves portions of Chapter 4.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 4 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The Kehatites carry the most holy objects — the ark, the table, the lampstand, the altars — but they carry them covered, never touching the sacred furniture directly. Contact with the uncovered holy objects brings death. The Levitical transport system is engineered around this principle: proximity to YHWH's furnishings requires covering, mediation, and exact procedure. The same logic that governs who can enter the Most Holy Place on Yom Kippur governs how the Mishkan's contents are transported through the wilderness.

THE LAWS IN THE WILDERNESS — CHAPTERS 5 THROUGH 10

BAMIDBAR — CHAPTER 5 Purity of the Camp — Restitution — The Sotah — Suspected Adultery

5:1 YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

5:2 Command the people of Yisra'EL that they put out of the camp everyone who is leprous or has a discharge and everyone who is unclean through contact with the dead.

5:3 You shall put out both male and female, putting them outside the camp, that they may not defile their camp, in the midst of which I dwell.

5:5 And YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

5:6 Speak to the people of Yisra'EL: When a man or woman commits any of the sins that people commit by breaking faith with YHWH, and that person realizes his guilt,

5:7 He shall confess his sin that he has committed. And he shall make full restitution for his wrong, adding a fifth to it and giving it to him to whom he did the wrong.

5:11 And YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

5:12 Speak to the people of Yisra'EL, saying: If any man's wife goes astray and breaks faith with him,

5:14 And if the spirit of jealousy comes over him and he is jealous of his wife who has defiled herself, or if the spirit of jealousy comes over him and he is jealous of his wife, though she has not defiled herself —

5:15 Then the man shall bring his wife to the priest and bring the offering required of her, a tenth of an ephah of barley flour. He shall pour no oil on it and put no frankincense on it, for it is a grain offering of jealousy, a grain offering of remembrance, bringing iniquity to remembrance.

5:29 This is the law in cases of jealousy, when a wife, though under her husband's authority, goes astray and defiles herself,

5:30 Or when the spirit of jealousy comes over a man and he is jealous of his wife. Then he shall set the woman before YHWH, and the priest shall carry out for her all this law.

5:31 The man shall be free from iniquity, but the woman shall bear her iniquity.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QNuma preserves portions of Chapter 5.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 5 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 5:3 — put them outside the camp, that they may not defile their camp, in the midst of which I dwell. The purity of the camp is required because YHWH dwells in its midst. This is the same principle stated in Vayikra 15:31. The wilderness camp is a sacred space structured around the Mishkan at its center, and the holiness of the center radiates outward as a requirement on the entire community. Exclusion from the camp during ritual impurity is not punishment — it is the protective boundary maintained by the covenant community around YHWH's dwelling.

BAMIDBAR — CHAPTER 6 The Nazirite Vow — The Priestly Blessing

6:1 And YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

6:2 Speak to the people of Yisra'EL and say to them: When either a man or a woman makes a special vow, the vow of a Nazirite, to separate himself to YHWH,

6:3 He shall separate himself from wine and strong drink. He shall drink no vinegar made from wine or strong drink and shall not drink any juice of grapes or eat grapes, fresh or dried.

6:5 All the days of his vow of separation, no razor shall touch his head. Until the time is completed for which he separates himself to YHWH, he shall be holy. He shall let the locks of hair of his head grow long.

6:6 All the days that he separates himself to YHWH he shall not go near a dead body.

6:21 This is the law of the Nazirite. But if he vows an offering to YHWH above his Nazirite vow, as he can afford, in addition to what the law of the Nazirite requires, so he shall do in keeping with the vow that he takes.

6:22 YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

6:23 Speak to Aharon and his sons, saying: Thus you shall bless the people of Yisra'EL — you shall say to them:

6:24 YHWH bless you and keep you.

6:25 YHWH make his face shine on you and be gracious to you.

6:26 YHWH lift up his countenance upon you and give you shalom.

6:27 So shall they put my name upon the people of Yisra'EL, and I will bless them.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QNuma and 4QNumb preserve portions of Chapter 6. The Priestly Blessing of verses 6:24-26 is one of the most significant textual finds in the DSS corpus. The blessing was discovered inscribed on two small silver scrolls at Ketef Hinnom in Yerushalayim — dated to the late First Temple period, making them the oldest known text of any portion of the Torah in existence, predating the Dead Sea Scrolls themselves. The Ketef Hinnom text confirms the Priestly Blessing was in use before the Babylonian exile.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 6 in full including the complete Priestly Blessing.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter. The Priestly Blessing text is identical across DSS, Ketef Hinnom, and Masoretic.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verses 6:24-26 — the Priestly Blessing. Three lines. Six verbs — bless, keep, shine, be gracious, lift up, give. YHWH's name appears three times — once in each line. The structure is ascending — four Hebrew words in the first line, five in the second, seven in the third. The blessing moves from protection to grace to peace — shalom — the covenant word that encompasses wholeness, completeness, right relationship, and well-being. YHWH's name is placed on the people through this blessing. Verse 6:27 states the purpose: so shall they put my name upon the people of Yisra'EL. The blessing is not a wish — it is the priestly act of naming the people with YHWH's name.

BAMIDBAR — CHAPTER 7 The Offerings of the Twelve Tribal Leaders — The Dedication of the Altar

7:1 On the day when Moshe had finished setting up the Mishkan and had anointed and consecrated it with all its furnishings and had anointed and consecrated the altar with all its utensils,

7:2 The chiefs of Yisra'EL, heads of their fathers' houses, who were the chiefs of the tribes, who were over those who were listed, approached

7:3 And brought their offerings before YHWH, six wagons and twelve oxen, a wagon for every two of the chiefs and for each one an ox. They brought them before the Mishkan.

7:10 And the chiefs offered offerings for the dedication of the altar on the day it was anointed — and the chiefs offered their offering before the altar.

7:12 He who offered his offering the first day was Nakhshon the son of Amminadav, of the tribe of Yehudah.

7:84 This was the dedication offering for the altar on the day when it was anointed, from the chiefs of Yisra'EL: twelve silver plates, twelve silver basins, twelve golden dishes.

7:89 And when Moshe went into the tent of meeting to speak with YHWH, he heard the voice speaking to him from above the mercy seat that was on the ark of the testimony, from between the two cherubim — and it spoke to him.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QNuma and 4QNumb preserve portions of Chapter 7.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 7 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 7:89 — Moshe heard the voice speaking to him from above the mercy seat, from between the two cherubim. The closing verse of the longest chapter in the Torah — Chapter 7 lists twelve identical offerings from twelve tribal leaders across twelve days — is a single verse describing YHWH speaking to Moshe from between the cherubim above the ark. The chapter of human dedication ends with YHWH's voice. The mercy seat — the kapporet — is confirmed here as the specific location of YHWH's speech to Moshe, consistent with YHWH's declaration in Shemot 25:22.

BAMIDBAR — CHAPTER 8 The Lamps of the Menorah — The Levites Consecrated

8:1 And YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

8:2 Speak to Aharon and say to him: When you set up the lamps, the seven lamps shall give light in front of the lampstand.

8:3 And Aharon did so — he set up its lamps in front of the lampstand, as YHWH commanded Moshe.

8:5 And YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

8:6 Take the Levites from among the people of Yisra'EL and cleanse them.

8:10 And you shall bring the Levites before YHWH, and the people of Yisra'EL shall lay their hands on the Levites.

8:11 And Aharon shall offer the Levites before YHWH as a wave offering from the people of Yisra'EL, that they may do the service of YHWH.

8:14 Thus you shall separate the Levites from among the people of Yisra'EL, and the Levites shall be mine.

8:16 For they are wholly given to me from among the people of Yisra'EL. Instead of all who open the womb, the firstborn of all the people of Yisra'EL, I have taken them for myself.

8:24 This applies to the Levites: from twenty-five years old and upward they shall come to do duty in the service of the tent of meeting.

8:25 And from the age of fifty years they shall withdraw from the duty of the service and serve no more.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QNuma preserves portions of Chapter 8.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 8 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The wave offering of the Levites before YHWH — verse 8:11 — uses the same gesture as the wave offerings of Vayikra: lifted and moved before YHWH as an act of presentation and dedication. The entire tribe of Levi is presented to YHWH as a living offering by the congregation of Yisra'EL. The people lay hands on the Levites — the same gesture of identification used in the sacrificial system — and Aharon waves them before YHWH. The Levitical consecration mirrors the offering system at every point.

BAMIDBAR — CHAPTER 9 The Second Pesach — The Cloud and Fire — When to Journey

9:1 And YHWH spoke to Moshe in the wilderness of Sinai, in the first month of the second year after they had come out of the land of Egypt, saying:

9:2 Let the people of Yisra'EL keep the Pesach at its appointed time.

9:6 And there were certain men who were unclean through touching a dead body, so that they could not keep the Pesach on that day, and they came before Moshe and Aharon on that day.

9:7 And those men said to him: We are unclean through touching a dead body. Why are we kept from bringing YHWH's offering at its appointed time among the people of Yisra'EL?

9:8 And Moshe said to them: Wait, that I may hear what YHWH will command concerning you.

9:9 YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

9:10 Speak to the people of Yisra'EL, saying: If any one of you or of your descendants is unclean through touching a dead body, or is on a long journey, he shall still keep the Pesach to YHWH.

9:11 In the second month on the fourteenth day at twilight they shall keep it. They shall eat it with unleavened bread and bitter herbs.

9:15 On the day that the Mishkan was set up, the cloud covered the Mishkan, the tent of the testimony. And at evening it was over the Mishkan like the appearance of fire until morning.

9:17 And whenever the cloud lifted from over the tent, after that the people of Yisra'EL set out, and in the place where the cloud settled down, there the people of Yisra'EL camped.

9:18 At the command of YHWH the people of Yisra'EL set out, and at the command of YHWH they camped. As long as the cloud rested over the Mishkan, they remained in camp.

9:22 Whether it was two days, or a month, or a longer time, that the cloud continued over the Mishkan, abiding there, the people of Yisra'EL remained in camp and did not set out, but when it lifted they set out.

9:23 At the command of YHWH they camped, and at the command of YHWH they set out. They kept the charge of YHWH, at the command of YHWH by Moshe.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QNuma and 4QNumb preserve portions of Chapter 9. The provision for a second Pesach — Pesach Sheni — in verses 9:10-11 is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 9 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 9:8 — wait, that I may hear what YHWH will command concerning you. Moshe's response when confronted with a legal question he cannot answer from existing legislation is to wait and ask YHWH. This is the pattern of Torah expansion in Bamidbar — novel situations arise in the wilderness, Moshe brings them to YHWH, and YHWH gives new legislation. The Torah is not a closed fixed code applied mechanically — it is a living covenant conversation between YHWH and the community through Moshe. The Pesach Sheni provision comes into existence because specific men asked a specific question in a specific situation.

BAMIDBAR — CHAPTER 10 The Silver Trumpets — The Departure from Sinai

10:1 YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

10:2 Make two silver trumpets. Of hammered work you shall make them, and you shall use them for summoning the congregation and for breaking camp.

10:9 And when you go to war in your land against the adversary who oppresses you, then you shall sound an alarm with the trumpets, that you may be remembered before YHWH your Creator, and you shall be saved from your enemies.

10:10 On the day of your gladness also, and at your appointed feasts and at the beginnings of your months, you shall blow the trumpets over your burnt offerings and over the sacrifices of your peace offerings. They shall be a reminder of you before your Creator — I am YHWH your Creator.

10:11 In the second year, in the second month, on the twentieth day of the month, the cloud lifted from over the Mishkan of the testimony.

10:12 And the people of Yisra'EL set out by stages from the wilderness of Sinai. And the cloud settled down in the wilderness of Paran.

10:29 And Moshe said to Hovav the son of Re'uel the Midyanite, Moshe's father-in-law: We are setting out for the place of which YHWH said: I will give it to you. Come with us, and we will do good to you, for YHWH has promised good to Yisra'EL.

10:33 So they set out from the mount of YHWH three days' journey. And the ark of the covenant of YHWH went before them three days' journey, to seek out a resting place for them.

10:35 And whenever the ark set out, Moshe said: Arise, O YHWH, and let your enemies be scattered, and let those who hate you flee before you.

10:36 And when it rested, he said: Return, O YHWH, to the ten thousand thousands of Yisra'EL.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QNuma and 4QNumb preserve portions of Chapter 10. The departure from Sinai and the ark-sayings of verses 10:35-36 are among the most attested passages in the Cave 4 Numbers manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 10 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verses 10:35-36 — arise, O YHWH, and let your enemies be scattered — return, O YHWH, to the ten thousand thousands of Yisra'EL. These two verses are marked in the Masoretic text with inverted Nun symbols before and after them — a unique scribal marker found nowhere else in the Torah. The DSS manuscripts confirm the text. Ancient scribal tradition recognized these verses as a distinct literary unit. They are the travel liturgy of the ark — spoken at every departure and every resting. YHWH travels with his people. The ark is not a container for sacred objects — it is the throne-chariot of YHWH moving through the wilderness.

THE REBELLIONS IN THE WILDERNESS — CHAPTERS 11 THROUGH 14

BAMIDBAR — CHAPTER 11 The Complaining — The Quail — Eldad and Medad — The Seventy Elders

11:1 And the people complained in the hearing of YHWH about their misfortunes, and when YHWH heard it, his anger was kindled, and the fire of YHWH burned among them and consumed some outlying parts of the camp.

11:4 Now the rabble that was among them had a strong craving. And the people of Yisra'EL also wept again and said: Oh that we had meat to eat.

11:5 We remember the fish we ate in Egypt that cost nothing, the cucumbers, the melons, the leeks, the onions, and the garlic.

11:6 But now our strength is dried up, and there is nothing at all but this manna to look at.

11:10 Moshe heard the people weeping throughout their clans, everyone at the door of his tent. And the anger of YHWH blazed hotly, and Moshe was displeased.

11:11 Moshe said to YHWH: Why have you dealt ill with your servant? And why have I not found favor in your sight, that you lay the burden of all this people on me?

11:14 I am not able to carry all this people alone — the burden is too heavy for me.

11:15 If you will treat me like this, kill me at once, if I find favor in your sight, that I may not see my wretchedness.

11:16 Then YHWH said to Moshe: Gather for me seventy men of the elders of Yisra'EL, whom you know to be the elders of the people and officers over them, and bring them to the tent of meeting, and let them take their stand there with you.

11:17 And I will come down and talk with you there. And I will take some of the Spirit that is on you and put it on them, and they shall bear the burden of the people with you, so that you may not bear it yourself alone.

11:24 So Moshe went out and told the people the words of YHWH. And he gathered seventy men of the elders of the people and placed them around the tent.

11:25 Then YHWH came down in the cloud and spoke to him, and took some of the Spirit that was on him and put it on the seventy elders. And as soon as the Spirit rested on them, they prophesied. But they did not continue doing it.

11:26 Now two men remained in the camp, one named Eldad, and the other named Medad, and the Spirit rested on them. They were among those registered, but they had not gone out to the tent, and so they prophesied in the camp.

11:28 And Yehoshua the son of Nun, the assistant of Moshe from his youth, said: My lord Moshe, stop them.

11:29 But Moshe said to him: Are you jealous for my sake? Would that all YHWH's people were prophets, that YHWH would put his Spirit on them.

11:31 Then a wind from YHWH sprang up, and it brought quail from the sea and let them fall beside the camp, about a day's journey on this side and a day's journey on the other side, around the camp, about two cubits above the ground.

11:33 While the meat was yet between their teeth, before it was consumed, the anger of YHWH was kindled against the people, and YHWH struck down the people with a very great plague.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QNuma and 4QNumb preserve portions of Chapter 11. The account of the seventy elders and Eldad and Medad is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 11 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 11:29 — would that all YHWH's people were prophets, that YHWH would put his Spirit on them. Moshe's response to Eldad and Medad prophesying outside the designated tent is the opposite of institutional gatekeeping. He expresses the wish that YHWH would democratize prophecy — extend his Spirit to all the people. The vision of the Spirit poured out on all flesh is anticipated here by Moshe himself, long before it appears in Yo'el 3:1. Moshe does not protect his prophetic monopoly. He opens his hands toward its expansion.

BAMIDBAR — CHAPTER 12 Miryam and Aharon Challenge Moshe — Moshe's Unique Standing

12:1 Miryam and Aharon spoke against Moshe because of the Kushite woman whom he had married, for he had married a Kushite woman.

12:2 And they said: Has YHWH indeed spoken only through Moshe? Has he not spoken through us also? And YHWH heard it.

12:3 Now the man Moshe was very meek, more than all people who were on the face of the earth.

12:4 And suddenly YHWH said to Moshe and to Aharon and Miryam: Come out, you three, to the tent of meeting. And the three of them came out.

12:5 And YHWH came down in a pillar of cloud and stood at the entrance of the tent and called Aharon and Miryam, and they both came forward.

12:6 And he said: Hear my words: If there is a prophet among you, I YHWH make myself known to him in a vision — I speak with him in a dream.

12:7 Not so with my servant Moshe. He is faithful in all my house.

12:8 With him I speak mouth to mouth, clearly, and not in riddles, and he beholds the form of YHWH. Why then were you not afraid to speak against my servant Moshe?

12:9 And the anger of YHWH was kindled against them, and he departed.

12:10 When the cloud removed from over the tent, behold, Miryam was leprous, like snow. And Aharon turned toward Miryam, and behold, she was leprous.

12:13 And Moshe cried to YHWH: O YHWH, please heal her — please.

12:14 But YHWH said to Moshe: If her father had but spit in her face, should she not be shamed seven days? Let her be shut outside the camp seven days, and after that she may be brought in again.

12:15 So Miryam was shut outside the camp seven days, and the people did not set out on the march till Miryam was brought in again.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QNuma preserves portions of Chapter 12. The account of Moshe's Kushite wife and YHWH's defense of Moshe's unique prophetic standing are attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 12 in full. The Orit and Ethiopian Ge'ez tradition identify Moshe's Kushite wife as Tzipporah of Midyan or as a second wife from the Kush region — the text states Kushite plainly and the Orit preserves this without harmonization.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 12:1 — Moshe married a Kushite woman. Kush in the ancient Hebrew text refers to the northeastern African territory south of Egypt — the region of Nubia, encompassing what is today Ethiopia, Sudan, and the upper Nile valley. This is consistent with the geographical use of Kush throughout the Nevi'im and Ketuvim. The challenge from Miryam and Aharon is provoked by this marriage. YHWH's response is not to address the marriage at all — he defends Moshe's unique prophetic standing and strikes Miryam with tzara'at. The ancient record does not condemn the Kushite marriage. YHWH does not condemn it. The text records the challenge and YHWH's response.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verses 12:6-8 — not so with my servant Moshe. He is faithful in all my house. With him I speak mouth to mouth, clearly, not in riddles, and he beholds the form of YHWH. YHWH himself declares the category difference between Moshe and all other prophets. Other prophets receive visions and dreams — mediated communication. Moshe receives direct speech — mouth to mouth — and beholds YHWH's form. Devarim 34:10 restates this: there has not arisen a prophet since in Yisra'EL like Moshe, whom YHWH knew face to face. This is the ancient record's own assessment of Moshe's standing — stated by YHWH directly.

BAMIDBAR — CHAPTER 13 The Twelve Spies Sent to Kena'an — The Report

13:1 YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

13:2 Send men to spy out the land of Kena'an, which I am giving to the people of Yisra'EL. From each tribe of their fathers you shall send a man, every one a chief among them.

13:6 From the tribe of Yehudah, Kalev the son of Yefunneh.

13:8 From the tribe of Efrayim, Hoshea the son of Nun.

13:16 These were the names of the men whom Moshe sent to spy out the land. And Moshe called Hoshea the son of Nun Yehoshua.

13:17 Moshe sent them to spy out the land of Kena'an and said to them: Go up into the Negev and go up into the hill country,

13:20 And whether the land is rich or poor, and whether there are trees in it or not. Be of good courage and bring some of the fruit of the land. Now the time was the season of the first ripe grapes.

13:23 And they came to the Valley of Eshkol and cut down from there a branch with a single cluster of grapes, and they carried it on a pole between two of them. They also brought some pomegranates and figs.

13:25 At the end of forty days they returned from spying out the land.

13:27 And they told him: We came to the land to which you sent us. It flows with milk and honey, and this is its fruit.

13:28 However, the people who dwell in the land are strong, and the cities are fortified and very large. And besides, we saw the descendants of Anak there.

13:30 But Kalev quieted the people before Moshe and said: Let us go up at once and occupy it, for we are well able to overcome it.

13:31 Then the men who had gone up with him said: We are not able to go up against the people, for they are stronger than we are.

13:32 So they brought to the people of Yisra'EL a bad report of the land that they had spied out, saying: The land, through which we have gone to spy it out, is a land that devours its inhabitants, and all the people that we saw in it are of great height.

13:33 And there we saw the Nephilim — the sons of Anak, who come from the Nephilim — and we seemed to ourselves like grasshoppers, and so we seemed to them.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QNuma and 4QNumb preserve portions of Chapter 13. The account of the twelve spies and the reference to the Nephilim in verse 13:33 are attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 13 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 13:33 — there we saw the Nephilim — the sons of Anak, who come from the Nephilim. The spies' report identifies the inhabitants of Kena'an as Nephilim — the same term used in Bereshit 6:4 for the offspring of the Watchers and human women. The Chanokh literature physically confirmed at Qumran provides the most extensive ancient treatment of the Nephilim as the giant offspring of the fallen Watchers. The ten fearful spies identify their enemies as the descendants of those beings. Whether this is accurate geography or psychological exaggeration by frightened men, the ancient record preserves the identification as stated.

BAMIDBAR — CHAPTER 14 The People Rebel — YHWH's Sentence — Forty Years

14:1 Then all the congregation raised a loud cry, and the people wept that night.

14:2 And all the people of Yisra'EL grumbled against Moshe and Aharon. The whole congregation said to them: Would that we had died in the land of Egypt. Or would that we had died in this wilderness.

14:4 And they said to one another: Let us choose a leader and go back to Egypt.

14:6 And Yehoshua the son of Nun and Kalev the son of Yefunneh, who were among those who had spied out the land, tore their clothes

14:7 And said to all the congregation of the people of Yisra'EL: The land, which we passed through to spy it out, is an exceedingly good land.

14:8 If YHWH delights in us, he will bring us into this land and give it to us, a land that flows with milk and honey.

14:9 Only do not rebel against YHWH. And do not fear the people of the land, for they are bread for us. Their protection is removed from them, and YHWH is with us. Do not fear them.

14:11 And YHWH said to Moshe: How long will this people despise me? And how long will they not believe in me, in spite of all the signs that I have done among them?

14:13 But Moshe said to YHWH: Then the Egyptians will hear of it, for you brought up this people in your might from among them.

14:17 And now, please let the power of YHWH be great as you have promised, saying:

14:18 YHWH is slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love, forgiving iniquity and transgression, but he will by no means clear the guilty, visiting the iniquity of the fathers on the children, to the third and the fourth generation.

14:19 Please pardon the iniquity of this people, according to the greatness of your steadfast love, just as you have forgiven this people, from Egypt until now.

14:20 Then YHWH said: I have pardoned, according to your word.

14:21 But truly, as I live, and as all the earth shall be filled with the glory of YHWH,

14:22 None of the men who have seen my glory and my signs that I did in Egypt and in the wilderness, and yet have put me to the test these ten times and have not obeyed my voice,

14:23 Shall see the land that I swore to give to their fathers. And none of those who despised me shall see it.

14:24 But my servant Kalev, because he has a different spirit and has followed me fully, I will bring into the land into which he went, and his descendants shall possess it.

14:29 Your dead bodies shall fall in this wilderness, and of all your number, listed in the census from twenty years old and upward, who have grumbled against me,

14:30 Not one shall come into the land where I swore that I would make you dwell, except Kalev the son of Yefunneh and Yehoshua the son of Nun.

14:33 And your children shall be shepherds in the wilderness forty years and shall suffer for your faithlessness, until the last of your dead bodies lies in the wilderness.

14:34 According to the number of the days in which you spied out the land, forty days, a year for each day, you shall bear your iniquity forty years, and you shall know my displeasure.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QNuma and 4QNumb preserve portions of Chapter 14. The forty-year sentence and the intercession of Moshe citing the Thirteen Attributes from Shemot 34:6-7 are attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 14 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14:18 — Moshe quotes the Thirteen Attributes of YHWH from Shemot 34:6-7 in his intercession. He uses YHWH's own self-declaration as the ground of his appeal for mercy. This is the second time in the Torah that Moshe intercedes using the covenant character of YHWH himself as his argument — the first was at the golden calf in Shemot 32 where he appealed to the Avrahamic covenant oath. The ancient record presents Moshe as a master of covenant argumentation before YHWH.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14:20-23 — I have pardoned, according to your word. But none of those who have tested me shall see the land. YHWH grants Moshe's request for pardon — the people are not destroyed. But the covenant consequence of the rebellion remains: the generation that refused to enter the land will not enter it. Pardon and consequence coexist. The Exodus generation is forgiven and still excluded from the promise. Their children will inherit what they refused.

THE LAWS AND REBELLIONS — CHAPTERS 15 THROUGH 19

BAMIDBAR — CHAPTER 15 Supplementary Offering Laws — The Fringes — The Sabbath Violation

15:1 YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

15:2 Speak to the people of Yisra'EL and say to them: When you come into the land you are to inhabit, which I am giving you,

15:3 And you offer to YHWH a food offering or a burnt offering or a sacrifice, to fulfill a vow or as a freewill offering or at your appointed feasts, to make a pleasing aroma to YHWH, from the herd or from the flock,

15:14 And if a stranger is sojourning with you, or anyone is living permanently among you, and he wishes to offer a food offering with a pleasing aroma to YHWH, he shall do as you do.

15:15 For the assembly, there shall be one statute for you and for the stranger who sojourns with you, a statute forever throughout your generations. You and the sojourner shall be alike before YHWH.

15:22 But if you sin unintentionally, and do not observe all these commandments that YHWH has spoken to Moshe —

15:27 If one person sins unintentionally, he shall offer a female goat a year old for a sin offering.

15:30 But the person who does anything with a high hand, whether he is native or a sojourner, reviles YHWH, and that person shall be cut off from among his people.

15:32 While the people of Yisra'EL were in the wilderness, they found a man gathering sticks on the Sabbath day.

15:35 And YHWH said to Moshe: The man shall be put to death — all the congregation shall stone him with stones outside the camp.

15:38 Speak to the people of Yisra'EL, and tell them to make tassels on the corners of their garments throughout their generations, and to put a cord of blue on the tassel of each corner.

15:39 And it shall be a tassel for you to look at and remember all the commandments of YHWH, to do them, not to follow after your own heart and your own eyes, which you are inclined to whore after.

15:40 So you shall remember and do all my commandments, and be holy to your Creator.

15:41 I am YHWH your Creator, who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your Creator — I am YHWH your Creator.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QNuma and 4QNumb preserve portions of Chapter 15. The tzitzit — fringes — legislation in verses 15:38-40 is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts and is also found in an expanded form in 4QTemple (11QTemple), the Temple Scroll from Cave 11.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 15 in full. The Ethiopian Beta Israel community observes the tzitzit legislation as documented ancient practice.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 15:38-40 — tassels on the corners of garments with a cord of blue, to look at and remember all the commandments. The tzitzit are a physical memory device woven into the garment itself. The blue cord — tekhelet — requires a specific dye derived from a sea creature. The fringes are worn at the four corners of the outer garment. Every time the wearer looks down, the covenant obligations are visible. The Torah embeds covenant memory into clothing — the most intimate physical layer of daily life. The commandments are not only written on tablets and scrolls. They are worn on the body.

BAMIDBAR — CHAPTER 16 The Rebellion of Korakh — The Earth Opens

16:1 Now Korakh the son of Yitzhar, son of Kehat, son of Levi, and Datan and Aviram the sons of Eli'av, and On the son of Pelet, sons of Re'uven, took men.

16:2 And they rose up before Moshe, with a number of the people of Yisra'EL, 250 chiefs of the congregation, chosen from the assembly, well-known men.

16:3 They assembled themselves together against Moshe and against Aharon and said to them: You have gone too far. For all in the congregation are holy, every one of them, and YHWH is among them. Why then do you exalt yourselves above the assembly of YHWH?

16:4 When Moshe heard it, he fell on his face.

16:9 Is it too small a thing for you that the Creator of Yisra'EL has separated you from the congregation of Yisra'EL, to bring you near to himself, to do service in the Mishkan of YHWH and to stand before the congregation to minister to them?

16:12 And Moshe sent to call Datan and Aviram the sons of Eli'av, and they said: We will not come up.

16:13 Is it a small thing that you have brought us up out of a land flowing with milk and honey, to kill us in the wilderness, that you must also make yourself a prince over us?

16:28 And Moshe said: Hereby you shall know that YHWH has sent me to do all these works, and that it has not been of my own accord.

16:29 If these men die as all men die, or if they are visited by the fate of all mankind, then YHWH has not sent me.

16:30 But if YHWH creates something new, and the ground opens its mouth and swallows them up with all that belongs to them, and they go down alive into Sheol, then you shall know that these men have despised YHWH.

16:31 And as soon as he had finished speaking all these words, the ground under them split apart.

16:32 And the earth opened its mouth and swallowed them up, with their households and all the people who belonged to Korakh and all their goods.

16:33 So they and all that belonged to them went down alive into Sheol, and the earth closed over them, and they perished from the midst of the assembly.

16:35 And fire came out from YHWH and consumed the 250 men offering the incense.

16:46 And Moshe said to Aharon: Take your censer, and put fire on it from off the altar and lay incense on it and carry it quickly to the congregation and make atonement for them, for wrath has gone out from YHWH — the plague has begun.

16:48 And he stood between the dead and the living, and the plague was stopped.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QNuma and 4QNumb preserve portions of Chapter 16. The rebellion of Korakh and the earth swallowing the rebels are attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 16 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 16:3 — all in the congregation are holy, every one of them, and YHWH is among them. Why do you exalt yourselves? Korakh's challenge uses true theological premises — the congregation is holy, YHWH is among them — to reach a false conclusion: therefore no distinction of role or function within the covenant community is legitimate. The ancient record does not dispute the holiness of the congregation. It disputes the implication that holiness eliminates differentiation of service. YHWH himself assigned Moshe and Aharon their roles. Challenging them is challenging the assignment of YHWH, not merely the authority of two men.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 16:48 — Aharon stood between the dead and the living, and the plague was stopped. Aharon — whose sons were consumed by YHWH's fire in Vayikra 10, who faced Korakh's challenge to his priestly authority in this chapter — now physically plants himself between the plague and the living people with a censer of atonement incense. His priesthood, confirmed through the deaths at the Mishkan's inauguration and through Korakh's destruction, is now the instrument that stops the plague. Atonement is made between the dead and the living by a man who has personally experienced both YHWH's fire and his mercy.

BAMIDBAR — CHAPTER 17 Aharon's Staff Buds — The Confirmation of the Levitical Priesthood

17:1 YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying:

17:2 Speak to the people of Yisra'EL, and get from them staffs, one for each fathers' house, from all their chiefs according to their fathers' houses, twelve staffs. Write each man's name on his staff.

17:3 And write Aharon's name on the staff of Levi. For there shall be one staff for the head of each fathers' house.

17:5 And the man whom I choose shall bud his staff shall sprout. Thus I will make to cease from me the grumblings of the people of Yisra'EL, which they grumble against you.

17:7 And Moshe deposited the staffs before YHWH in the tent of the testimony.

17:8 On the next day Moshe went into the tent of the testimony, and behold, the staff of Aharon for the house of Levi had sprouted and put forth buds and produced blossoms, and it bore ripe almonds.

17:10 And YHWH said to Moshe: Put back the staff of Aharon before the testimony, to be kept as a sign for the rebels, that you may make an end of their grumblings against me, lest they die.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QNuma preserves portions of Chapter 17. Aharon's budding staff is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 17 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 17:8 — the staff of Aharon had sprouted and put forth buds and produced blossoms and bore ripe almonds. A dead wooden staff — cut from a tree, carrying no root, no soil, no water — produces the entire sequence of almond growth overnight: bud, blossom, ripe fruit. The sign is total and unambiguous. YHWH's confirmation of Aharon's priestly authority comes through life generated from a dead instrument. The twelve staffs are identical in every physical respect — only Aharon's blooms. The covenant chooses and YHWH demonstrates the choice visibly. The staff is kept before the ark as permanent testimony.

BAMIDBAR — CHAPTER 18 The Duties and Portions of the Priests and Levites

18:1 So YHWH said to Aharon: You and your sons and your father's house with you shall bear iniquity connected with the sanctuary, and you and your sons with you shall bear iniquity connected with your priesthood.

18:6 And behold, I have taken your brothers the Levites from among the people of Yisra'EL. They are a gift to you, given to YHWH, to do the service of the tent of meeting.

18:8 Then YHWH spoke to Aharon: Behold, I have given you charge of the contributions made to me, all the consecrated things of the people of Yisra'EL. I have given them to you as a portion and to your sons as a perpetual due.

18:19 All the holy contributions that the people of Yisra'EL present to YHWH I give to you, and to your sons and daughters with you, as a perpetual due. It is a covenant of salt forever before YHWH for you and for your offspring with you.

18:20 And YHWH said to Aharon: You shall have no inheritance in their land, neither shall you have any portion among them. I am your portion and your inheritance among the people of Yisra'EL.

18:21 To the Levites I have given every tithe in Yisra'EL for an inheritance, in return for their service that they do, their service in the tent of meeting.

18:28 So you shall also present a contribution to YHWH from all your tithes that you receive from the people of Yisra'EL. And from it you shall give YHWH's contribution to Aharon the priest.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QNuma preserves portions of Chapter 18.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 18 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 18:20 — I am your portion and your inheritance among the people of Yisra'EL. The Levites and the priests receive no territorial inheritance in the land. Every tribe receives land. Levi receives YHWH. This is the covenant arrangement: the tribe dedicated to YHWH's service is sustained by the contributions of all the other tribes. Their inheritance is not geography — it is the covenant relationship with YHWH itself. The Tehillim echo this: YHWH is my portion — as a statement of the deepest covenant contentment.

BAMIDBAR — CHAPTER 19 The Red Heifer — The Purification from Death

19:1 Now YHWH spoke to Moshe and to Aharon, saying:

19:2 This is the statute of the law that YHWH has commanded: Tell the people of Yisra'EL to bring you a red heifer without defect, in which there is no blemish, and on which a yoke has never come.

19:3 And you shall give it to El'azar the priest, and it shall be taken outside the camp and slaughtered before him.

19:4 And El'azar the priest shall take some of its blood with his finger and sprinkle some of its blood toward the front of the tent of meeting seven times.

19:5 And the heifer shall be burned in his sight. Its skin, its flesh, and its blood, with its dung, shall be burned.

19:9 And a man who is clean shall gather up the ashes of the heifer and deposit them outside the camp in a clean place. And they shall be kept for the water of purification for the congregation of the people of Yisra'EL — it is a sin offering.

19:11 Whoever touches the dead body of any person shall be unclean seven days.

19:12 He shall cleanse himself with the water on the third day and on the seventh day, and so be clean. But if he does not cleanse himself on the third day and on the seventh day, he will not become clean.

19:17 For the unclean they shall take some ashes of the burnt sin offering, and fresh water shall be added in a vessel.

19:18 Then a clean person shall take hyssop and dip it in the water and sprinkle it on the tent and on all the furnishings and on the persons who were there and on whoever touched the bone, or the slain or the dead or the grave.

19:20 But the man who is unclean and does not cleanse himself, that person shall be cut off from the midst of the assembly, since he has defiled the sanctuary of YHWH. Because the water for impurity has not been thrown on him, he is unclean.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QNuma and 4QNumb preserve portions of Chapter 19. The red heifer legislation is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts. The Mishnaic tractate Parah — which later codified the Rabbinic interpretation of the red heifer — was not found at Qumran, indicating the DSS community had its own understanding of this legislation independent of Rabbinic development.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 19 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The red heifer — parah adumah — is described in the ancient record as a chok — a statute whose reason is not given. The procedure is paradoxical: the ashes of the heifer purify the impure person but defile the clean person who prepares the purification water. The ancient wisdom tradition acknowledged that not every covenant requirement yields to rational analysis. The chok stands as given — YHWH commanded it. The purification from contact with death is one of the most serious impurity categories in Vayikra and Bamidbar. The red heifer provides the instrument of restoration. Its logic belongs to YHWH.

END OF BAMIDBAR PART ONE — VOLUME FOUR

Chapters 1 through 19 — Census — Camp — Laws — Rebellions — Purification

𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

CONTINUES IN BAMIDBAR PART TWO

Chapters 20 through 36 — The Final Years — The Border Victories — The Assignments
